channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: BREAKING: FG returns 13 oil blocks to licensing basket
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The Federal Government has announced that 13 oil blocks from the 2025 Licensing Round will be returned to the basket after failing to attract bids. Learn m
Read More: https://punchng.com/breaking-fg-returns-13-oil-blocks-to-licensing-basket/

item.title: 2027: Ogun chiefs, retirees back Yayi’s governorship bid
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Ogun State chiefs and retirees endorse Senator Solomon Adeola (Yayi) for the 2027 governorship election, citing his development record and vision for the s
Read More: https://punchng.com/2027-ogun-chiefs-retirees-back-yayis-governorship-bid/

item.title: Enugu gov celebrates 13-year-old STEM Olympiad gold medallist
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Enugu State Governor Peter Mbah celebrates 13-year-old Chimdiebube Onwubiko Victor, who won gold in Mathematics at the International STEM Olympiad.
Read More: https://punchng.com/enugu-gov-celebrates-13-year-old-stem-olympiad-gold-medallist/

item.title: Uzodimma approves N25bn judges’ quarters, N1.9bn CBT centres for Imo
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Imo State Governor Hope Uzodimma approves N25bn for judges’ quarters and N1.9bn for new CBT centres, enhancing welfare and digital infrastructure in Imo.
Read More: https://punchng.com/uzodimma-approves-n25bn-judges-quarters-n1-9bn-cbt-centres-for-imo/

item.title: JUST IN: Highest bidder won’t automatically get oil blocks — FG
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The Federal Government states that the highest financial bidder won’t automatically win oil blocks in the 2025 Licensing Round; technical competence is key
Read More: https://punchng.com/just-in-highest-bidder-wont-automatically-get-oil-blocks-fg/

item.title: PFIPC scandal: Gbajabiamila invited, not arrested – ICPC
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
ICPC clarifies that Chief of Staff Femi Gbajabiamila was invited, not arrested, for questioning regarding the Presidential Foreign Investment Promotion Cou
Read More: https://punchng.com/pfipc-scandal-gbajabiamila-invited-not-arrested-icpc/

item.title: Plateau gov apologises to Igbo over civil war role
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Plateau State Governor, Caleb Mutfwang, apologises to the Igbo community for the state’s role in the Nigerian Civil War, urging unity and prosperity.
Read More: https://punchng.com/plateau-gov-apologises-to-igbo-over-civil-war-role/

item.title: Soldiers kill Boko Haram fighter, foil levy collection in Yobe
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Nigerian troops foil Boko Haram/ISWAP levy collection in Yobe, killing one terrorist and recovering weapons. Learn how Operation HADIN KAI disrupts terror
Read More: https://punchng.com/soldiers-kill-boko-haram-fighter-foil-levy-collection-in-yobe/

item.title: Nnamdi Kanu’s lawyer threaten legal action over death call
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Nnamdi Kanu’s legal team condemns a call for his extrajudicial killing, threatening legal action against the individual and TV station involved.
Read More: https://punchng.com/nnamdi-kanus-lawyer-threaten-legal-action-over-death-call/

item.title: Africa Becomes Continent With Highest Population Facing Hunger – UN
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: Africa has been hit by severe climate-related shocks in recent years, and conflicts have aggravated the situation. The post Africa Becomes Continent With Highest Population Facing Hunger – UN appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: Israeli military eliminates top Hamas commander, Abu Tim
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Israeli military said it has killed Asma Kamal Shehadeh Abu Tim, whom it identified as a cell commander in Hamas’ military wing. In a statement posted on its official X account on Tuesday, the Israel Defense Forces, IDF, alleged that Abu Tim participated in the October 7, 2023, a

item.title: Lagos Flooding: Stakeholders Seek Probe Into Coastal Highway Alignment
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Chief Executive Officer of Winhomes Limited, Engr. Stella Okengwu, and chairman of Gtext Holdings, Mr Stephen Akintayo, have called for an independent investigation into whether the alignment and design of the Lagos-Calabar Coastal Highway contributed to the severe flooding recently experienced in p

item.title: ‘I’m depresed, but I won’t give up’: Chidimma reacts to South Africa deportation bid
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Reacting after the proceedings, Adetshina wrote on Instagram: “Exhausted — yes. Burnt out — yes. Depressed — yes. Will I give up? No! We no dey carry last. See you next month.”

item.title: Alphabet jumps 2.8%, adds $15 billion to Google founders’ wealth
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: Alphabet shares jumped 2.8% to above $356 on Monday, reversing a 6.5% decline over the previous two trading sessions, and adding a combined $15 billion to the net worths of cofounders Larry Page and Sergey Brin. The post Alphabet jumps 2.8%, adds $15 billion to Google founders’ wealth appeared

item.title: State of Tech in Africa H1 2026 recap: Is consolidation the new growth story?
item.dc:creator: TechCabal
item.description: Investors and founders were candid about why early-stage capital for African founders has dried up, what African venture capital owes the ecosystem, and where the next wave of growth is actually coming from at the launch of TechCabal Insights’ State of Tech in Africa (SOTIA) H1 2026 report. On

item.title: We have $8 million in traditional IRAs. Should we tap them to buy a house — and take the tax hit?
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: “We would rather pay taxes upfront than pay mortgage interest.”

item.title: UK/Africa: How Britain replaced aid cheques with project architecture
item.dc:creator: The Africa Report
item.description: Nigeria’s Lagos ports illustrate Britain’s new pitch to Africa: the UK’s new prime minister Andy Burnham inherits a development team that can make tough projects financeable, with guarantees and risk engineering.

item.title: Is MTN Launching Data Plan for Gamers?
item.dc:creator: TechEconomy
item.description: Khalamanja & Gamers Ask At Data on Trial Nigerian gamer and streamer Ayere-Victor Great Ehinome, popularly known as Khalamanja, raised a question many in Nigeria’s gaming and streaming community have long wanted answered during MTN Nigeria’s Data on Trial, held recently in Lagos. “A huge number

item.title: Be hopeful in spite of life’s challenges – Okonkwo tells youths
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Okonkwo also expressed her gratitude and and pride in her journey, urging the youths to take a cue from her story and be inspired by her journey.

item.title: PACE Coaches Clinic 2026 Returns To Raise The Standard Of Basketball Coaching In Nigeria
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: As Nigerian basketball continues to produce talents making their mark around the world, one challenge remains impossible to ignore: the need to invest in the coaches responsible for developing the next generation. That is the driving force behind the PACE Coaches Clinic, a free basketball coaching d

item.title: I Will Use Political Capital To Tackle Social Care – New UK PM
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: New Prime Minister of the United Kingdom (UK), Andy Burnham, has promised to use some of his political capital to tackle the nation’s social care crisis, stressing that this has been an issue close to his heart. Speaking to reporters yesterday, just hours after he entered his new Downing Street offi

item.title: Adobe stock has been crushed by AI fears. Now Morgan Stanley has cut its rating to underweight.
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Morgan Stanley cut its price target for Adobe by more than a third, citing multiple transitions and the potential for increased competition from artificial-intelligence alternatives.

item.title: 2027: PRP National Vice chairman, Olajide Epe resigns
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The National Vice Chairman, South West, of the Peoples Redemption Party, PRP Wolimoh Olajide Epe, has resigned from the party ahead of the 2027 general elections. In a resignation letter addressed to Hakeem Baba-Ahmed, the National Chairman of the PRP, Wolimoh cited concerns over what he described a

item.title: Kogi L-PRES Flags Off Awareness Campaign For Mass Artificial Insemination Program

item.description: The Kogi State Livestock Productivity and Resilience Support Project, Kogi L-PRES, has flagged off a statewide awareness campaign for the implementation of its Mass Artificial Insemination, AI, Program. The five-day sensitization exercise, which commenced on Monday, July 20th and will run till July

item.title: France could become first EU country to ban social media for under-15s
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: If the bill passes both the lower house and the senate, France is expected to become the first EU nation to ban social media for those under 15. Solange Mougin has more on the ban and how it wll work. Championed by Emmanuel Macron, it comes on the heels of similar bans in Australia and Indonesia and

item.title: What to know about the West African economic summit seeking regional reform

Yakubu, who made the call at a news conference in Abuja, yesterday, said th

item.title: WHO Raises Alarm As Wars, Hunger And Disease Push Eastern Mediterranean Towards Health Emergency
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The World Health Organisation (WHO) says a combination of armed conflicts, infectious disease outbreaks, worsening hunger and shrinking humanitarian budgets is creating an unprecedented health crisis across the Eastern Mediterranean. The UN agency warned that already fragile healthcare systems are s

item.title: Police Uncover Alleged Prisoner Linked To AK-47 Supply, Arrest Robbery Suspects
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Ondo State Police Command has uncovered an alleged prisoner linked to the supply of an AK-47 rifle following the arrest of three suspected members of an interstate armed robbery syndicate. They are believed to be responsible for a string of violent robberies across Ondo and Lagos States. The commiss

item.title: “Nigeria Has Happened to Me”: Rebuilding Agency in a land of Scarcity
item.dc:creator: TechEconomy
item.description: | By: Freedom Chimereze FORCE In the everyday lexicon of Nigerians, few phrases capture the sting of systemic frustration quite like “Nigeria has happened to me.” It surfaces after a power outage ruins perishable stock, a bureaucratic delay scuttles a business deal, inflation erodes

item.title: Zidane Reportedly Signs Deal to Become France Coach Until 2030
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: French football icon, Zinedine Zidane, has reportedly agreed a deal to become the next head coach of the French national team, with his contract set to run until June 2030. Transfer expert, Fabrizio Romano, disclosed the development in a post shared on Facebook on Tuesday, claiming the former Real M

item.title: Court Upholds FCCPC’s DEON Regulations, Affirms NCC’s Licensing Powers
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Federal High Court, Lagos Judicial Division, on Tuesday upheld the Direct Emergency Operator Notification (DEON) Regulations issued by the Federal Competition and Consumer Protection Commission (FCCPC). The court also ruled that the power to issue telecommunications licences rests solely with th

item.title: Fire destroys 17 shops in Lagos market

item.description: According to the agency, preliminary findings showed the fire started in one shop before spreading to adjoining shops. The post Fire destroys 17 shops in Lagos market appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.description: Abia State governor, Alex Otti, has further brightened the future of Master Michael Offorkaile, the Abia-born student who scored 367 in the 2026 JAMB/UTME, with offer of support. Offorkaile is the 5th highest scorer in the country and governor Otti while receiving the brilliant student, his parents,

item.description: Yobe State Police Command has arrested a 65-year-old man for allegedly having canal knowledge of an eight-year-old girl in the Bara area of the Gulani local government area of the state. The spokesperson of the command, SP Dungus Abdulkarim, disclosed this in a statement on Sunday in Damaturu. He sa

item.title: ‘Abacha Died Of Cardiac Arrest During Sexual Intercourse’ — Ex-DSS Operative Reveals

item.description: Dennis Amachree, a former assistant director of the Department of State Services (DSS), has shared his account of the final moments of Sani Abacha, ex-head of state. Abacha was Nigeria’s military ruler from November 1993 to June 1998. His passing on the throne has been shrouded in controversy and co

item.dc:creator: Leadership

item.title: One billion people battling mental illness, women worst hit — Nwadinobi
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Immediate Past President of the Medical Women’s International Association (MWIA), Dr Eleanor Nwadinobi, has raised the alarm that one billion people worldwide are living with mental health conditions, warning that women and girls bear a disproportionate share of the burden due to violence, dis

item.title: South Africa Secures $1.5bn World Bank Loan
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: South Africa has secured a $1.5 bn Development Policy Loan from the World Bank to support structural reforms in its electricity, freight logistics and water and sanitation sectors, as the government pushes to lift economic growth and create hundreds of thousands of jobs. The loan agreement, signed b

item.title: Australian MP interrupts tough interview to yell at dog
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Australian politician Barnaby Joyce interrupted a live TV interview to shout at his dog.

item.title: Everton agree deal to sign Leicester’s Rantala
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Everton agree a deal to sign Finland international Jutta Rantala from Leicester City.

item.title: New UK PM Burnham unveils tax cut after cabinet overhaul
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Britain’s new Prime Minister, Andy Burnham, has announced a cut in value-added tax (VAT) on household electricity bills as his first major policy decision, promising immediate relief for families struggling with the rising cost of living. The post New UK PM Burnham unveils tax cut after cabinet over

item.title: Kenya introduces mandatory licence for phone and router importers to curb counterfeits
item.dc:creator: Technext
item.description: The Communications Authority of Kenya (CA) has introduced a mandatory Communications Equipment Distributor (CED) Licence for all companies…

item.title: The New Insider Traders Lurking on Prediction Markets
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Sites like Polymarket can be rewarding for traders with inside info. There are different ways to get it.

item.title: LemFi and BVNK partner to rebuild the rails of the diaspora economy — on stablecoin rails
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: LemFi is moving its cross-border settlement onto stablecoin rails with BVNK, replacing multi-day correspondent banking chains with near-instant, lower-cost settlement. The partnership operationalises the stablecoin settlement strategy LemFi began with Tether’s USD₮ investment in May 2026, mark

item.title: 'Fight of the year!' | Lamine Yamal holds Paredes sign during bus parade!
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Lamine Yamal showed off a Paredes vs Gavi sign at Spain's open-top bus parade celebrating their World Cup win, referencing the scuffle between the two midfielders after the full-time whistle on Sunday.

item.title: ‘I have a $2,000 deductible’: I reported a cracked windshield on my Hertz rental car. Can they charge me?
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: “I never heard a rock hit the windshield.”

item.title: FRSC confirms two dead in Abeokuta auto crash
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The Federal Road Safety Corps (FRSC), Ogun Sector Command, says two persons have died in a road crash involving a Lexus car and a motorcycle at the Leme area of Abeokuta. The post FRSC confirms two dead in Abeokuta auto crash appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: World Cup 2026: Messi tops list of players with successful dribbles [Full list]
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Argentina captain, Lionel Messi, topped the list of players with successful dribbles at the 2026 FIFA World Cup. The 39-year-old forward finished number one on the list with 28 successful dribbles. The former Barcelona captain is ahead of Spain’s Lamine Yamal and England’s Jude Bellingha

item.title: Troops Foil Boko Haram Extortion Mission, Kill Terrorist In Yobe
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Troops of Operation HADIN KAI have foiled an attempt by suspected Boko Haram/ISWAP terrorists to extort residents in Yobe State, killing one insurgent and recovering weapons, ammunition and logistics items during a coordinated operation. The post Troops Foil Boko Haram Extortion Mission, Kill Terror

item.title: Court affirms FCCPC powers, raises questions over airtime credit providers’ regulatory status
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: A Federal High Court has affirmed the Federal Competition and Consumer Protection Commission’s (FCCPC) authority to regulate Nigeria’s digital consumer lending sector while raising fresh questions over the regulatory status of five firms approved by the Commission to provide airtime and
